idea.hook: Ever dream of visiting another star?
facts: Proxima Centauri, our nearest star, is 4.24 light-years away.
The fastest human-made object, the Parker Solar Probe, travels at ~692,000 km/h.
At the Parker Solar Probe's speed, reaching Proxima Centauri would take over 6,600 years.
Even at 99% the speed of light, the journey would still take 4.28 years.
idea.payoff: Interstellar travel, even to our closest neighbor, is a multi-generational journey at current speeds!
idea.title: Road Trip to Another Star? 🤯🌟

scenes.text: Ever dream of visiting another star?
scenes.visual: Stylized Earth slowly rotating against a backdrop of twinkling stars. A faint, glowing path extends from Earth into the distant cosmos.
A wide shot showing a small, distant Earth and a larger, reddish Proxima Centauri star. A sweeping line with the text '4.24 light-years' appears between them.

scenes.text: Fastest Human Object: Parker Solar Probe (692k km/h)
scenes.visual: A sleek, futuristic space probe (representing Parker Solar Probe) streaks across the screen from left to right, leaving a faint energy trail. Digital speed readout flashes.

scenes.text: At that speed? Over 6,600 years to Proxima Centauri!
scenes.visual: An animated timeline unfurls from left to right, showing 'START' then stretching into a vast expanse with '6,600+ YEARS' marked. Proxima Centauri glows faintly at the very far end.

scenes.text: 99% Light Speed: Still 4.28 Years! A Multi-Generational Journey.
scenes.visual: A vibrant, swirling 'wormhole' or light-speed tunnel effect fills the screen. As the text appears, silhouettes representing multiple generations (e.g., child, adult, elder) briefly flash and overlay the starfield, emphasizing the passage of time.
